mnemonics: SIGnal KILLs Daemons (SIGKILL is the signal that cannot be caught or ignored, used to terminate daemons)
D.A.E.M.O.N: Detach from terminal, Avoid zombie processes, Execute main logic, Manage signals, Operate in background, Notify status changes
keyPoints: Signals are software interrupts delivered to a process
Daemons are background processes that run independently of a controlling terminal
The fork() and setsid() system calls are crucial for creating a daemon
commonMistakes: Forgetting to ignore SIGHUP when daemonizing
Not properly closing inherited file descriptors
Failing to handle zombie processes by ignoring SIGCHLD
